notes.title: Variance
notes.content:
Variance is a measure of how spread out a set of data values is from the mean (average). It shows how much the values differ from the mean on average.

If the variance is small, the data points are close to the mean.
If the variance is large, the data points are widely spread out.

It is calculated by:

Finding the mean
Subtracting the mean from each value
Squaring the differences
Finding the average of those squared differences

Variance is important because it helps us understand the consistency and variability of data.
notes.subject: STAT 112
